title: Anisomeles albiflora
intro: Anisomeles albiflora, also known as the fragrant ginger, is a perennial shrub native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant leaves and white flowers. The health benefits of Anisomeles albiflora include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Anisomeles albiflora is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to treat respiratory problems and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Anisomeles albiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Anisomeles, which is a group of plants commonly known as the Indian smoke bush or Thai smoke bush. The family Lamiaceae, to which Anisomeles albiflora belongs, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the mint family. The Lamiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, but Anisomeles albiflora's specific classification within these groups is not well-documented. Anisomeles albiflora is native to Southeast Asia and is often used in traditional medicine.

Its leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases due to its analgesic and anti-pyretic properties. Additionally, it is used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and as a remedy for various skin conditions.
